info.title: Harry Potter API - With magic visualizations
info.description: # Introduction A simple JSON-based API for getting information on Harry Potter characters. Hosted at [https://hp-api.onrender.com.](https://hp-api.onrender.com.) GitHub repo [here](https://github.com/KostaSav/hp-api). # Overview - Using `GET All characters`, we can view everyone (people, animals, beasts) from the fantasy world of Harry Potter - Using `GET Hogwarts students` / `GET Hogwarts staff`, we can view characters who study or work at Hogwarts - Using `GET Characters in house`, we can view the particular characters who belong to one of the four houses (Gryffindor, Ravenclaw, Hufflepuff, Slytherin) # Visualization Every request comes with a nice viualization in Postman, you need to switch to the Visualization tab once you have sent a response. # Authentication No API key is required. # Error Codes - 404 Not Found: no path variable in `GET Characters in house`

example.name: Aberto
example.description: Opens locked doors

example.name: Accio
example.description: Summons objects

example.name: Aguamenti
example.description: Summons water

example.name: Alohomora
example.description: Unlocks objects

example.name: Anapneo
example.description: Clears someone's airway

example.name: Aparecium
example.description: Reveals secret written messages

example.name: Apparate
example.description: A non-verbal transportation spell that allows a witch or wizard to instantly travel on the spot and appear at another location (disapparate is the opposite)

example.name: Ascendio
example.description: Propells someone into the air

example.name: Avada Kedavra
example.description: Also known as The Killing Curse, the most evil spell in the Wizarding World; one of three Unforgivable Curses; Harry Potter is the only known witch or wizard to survive it

example.name: Avis
example.description: Conjures a small flock of birds

example.name: Bat
example.description: Bogey Hex - Turns the target's boogers into bats

example.name: Bombardo
example.description: Creates an explosion

example.name: Brackium Emendo
example.description: Heals broken bones

example.name: Capacious Extremis
example.description: Known as the Extension Charm, it's a complicated spell that can greatly expand or extend the capacity of an object or space without affecting it externally

example.name: Confundo
example.description: Known as the Confundus Charm, it causes confusion of the target

example.name: Conjunctivitis Curse
example.description: Affects the eyes and sight of a target

example.name: Crinus Muto
example.description: Changes hair color and style

example.name: Crucio
example.description: One of three Unforgivable Curses, it causes unbearable pain in the target

example.name: Diffindo
example.description: Used to precisely cut an object

example.name: Disillusionment Charm
example.description: Causes the target to take on the appearance of its surroundings

example.name: Disapparate
example.description: A non-verbal transportation spell that allows a witch or wizard to instantly travel on the spot and leave for another location (apparate is the opposite)

example.name: Engorgio
example.description: Causes rapid growth in the targeted object

example.name: Episkey
example.description: Heals minor injuries

example.name: Expecto patronum
example.description: The Patronus Charm is a powerful projection of hope and happiness that drives away Dementors; a corpeal Patronus takes the the respective animal form of the caster, while a non-corpeal appears as a wisp of light; at 13, Harry Potter was the youngest known witch or wizard to prouduce a corpeal Patronus

example.name: Erecto
example.description: Allows a witch or wizard to build a structure, like a tent

example.name: Evanesco
example.description: Vanishes objects

example.name: Expelliarmus
example.description: Forces an opponent to drop whatever's in their possession

example.name: Ferula
example.description: A healing charm that conjures wraps and bandages for wounds

example.name: Fidelius Charm
example.description: A complex charm that conceals a secret into the soul of a chosen "Secret Keeper". If a location is the subject of concealment, it becomes undetectable to others

example.name: Fiendfyre Curse
example.description: Conjures destructive, enormous enchanted flames

example.name: Finite Incantatem
example.description: A general counter-spell that's used to reverse or counter already cast charms

example.name: Furnunculus Curse
example.description: A jinx that causes a breakout of boils or pimples

example.name: Geminio
example.description: Duplicates objects

example.name: Glisseo
example.description: Transforms a staircase into a slide

example.name: Homenum Revelio
example.description: Reveals the presence of another person

example.name: Homonculus Charm
example.description: Detects anyone's true identity and location on a piece of parchment; used to create the Marauder's Map

example.name: Immobulus
example.description: Immobilises living targets

example.name: Impedimenta
example.description: A temporary jinx that slows the movement of the target

example.name: Incarcerous
example.description: Conjures ropes

example.name: Imperio
example.description: One of the three Unforgivable Curses, it places the target under the complete control of the caster

example.name: Impervius
example.description: Makes an object waterproof

example.name: Incendio
example.description: Conjures flames

example.name: Langlock
example.description: Causes the target's tongue to stick to the roof of their mouth

example.name: Legilimens
example.description: Invading or navigating another's mind

example.name: Levicorpus
example.description: Levitates the target by their ankle

example.name: Locomotor Mortis
example.description: The Leg-Locker curse bounds the target's legs

example.name: Lumos
example.description: Illuminates the caster's wand

example.name: Morsmordre
example.description: Conjures and projects Lord Voldemort's Dark Mark

example.name: Mucus Ad Nauseam
example.description: Inflicts an extreme runny nose or cold

example.name: Muffliato
example.description: Creates a buzzing sound in the target's ears to prevent eavesdropping

example.name: Nox
example.description: Reverses the lumos charm, extinguishing a wand's light

example.name: Obliviate
example.description: Erases the target's memory

example.name: Obscuro
example.description: Conjures a blindfold

example.name: Oculus Reparo
example.description: Repairs eyeglasses

example.name: Oppugno
example.description: Directs an object or person to attack a victim

example.name: Petrificus Totalus
example.description: Temporarily freezes or petrifies the body of the target

example.name: Periculum
example.description: Conjures flares/red sparks

example.name: Piertotum Locomotor
example.description: Incantation used to bring to life inanimate objects and artifacts

example.name: Protean Charm
example.description: Links objects together for better communication

example.name: Protego
example.description: Casts an invisible shield around the caster, protecting against spells and objects (except for The Killing Curse)

example.name: Reducto
example.description: Reduces the target to pieces

example.name: Reducio
example.description: Shrinks an enlarged object to its regular size

example.name: Renneverate
example.description: Awakens or revives the target

example.name: Reparifors
example.description: Heals magical ailments like poisoning or paralysis

example.name: Reparo
example.description: Fixes broken objects

example.name: Rictusempra
example.description: A charm that disarms an opponent by tickling them

example.name: Riddikulus
example.description: Used to defeat a Boggart, the charm allows the scary creature to assume a comedic form, disarming it

example.name: Scourgify
example.description: Cleans objects

example.name: Sectumsempra
example.description: Inflicts severe lacerations and haemorrhaging on the target

example.name: Serpensortia
example.description: Conjures a live snake

example.name: Silencio
example.description: Silences the target

example.name: Sonorus
example.description: Amplifies the witch or wizard's voice

example.name: Spongify
example.description: Softens the target

example.name: Stupefy
example.description: The Stunning spell freezes objects and renders living targets unconscious

example.name: Tarantallegra
example.description: Aimed at the legs, causes uncontrollable dancing movement

example.name: Unbreakable Vow
example.description: A magically binding contract that results in the death of whoever breaks it

example.name: Wingardium Leviosa
example.description: Causes an object to levitate; but remember what Hermione said: "It's Wing-gar-dium Levi-o-sa, make the 'gar' nice and long.'"

example.name: Mrs Norris
example.actor: Maxime, Alanis and Tommy the cats

get.description: view characters that belong to a specific Hogwarts house

example.name: Mrs Norris
example.actor: Maxime, Alanis and Tommy the cats

get.description:
## View All Characters

This API endpoint retrieves a list of characters from the database.

### Request

- **Method**: GET
    
- **URL**: `{{render}}/api/characters`
    

### Response

- **Status Code**: 200
    
- **Content-Type**: application/json
    

#### Response Body

The response will be a JSON array containing character objects with the following properties:

- **id**: Unique identifier for the character (string).
    
- **name**: Name of the character (string).
    
- **alternate_names**: Array of alternate names for the character (array of strings).
    
- **species**: Species of the character (string).
    
- **gender**: Gender of the character (string).
    
- **house**: House affiliation of the character (string).
    
- **dateOfBirth**: Date of birth of the character (string).
    
- **yearOfBirth**: Year of birth of the character (integer).
    
- **wizard**: Indicates if the character is a wizard (boolean).
    
- **ancestry**: Ancestry of the character (string).
    
- **eyeColour**: Eye color of the character (string).
    
- **hairColour**: Hair color of the character (string).
    
- **wand**: Object containing wand details:
    
    - **wood**: Wood type of the wand (string).
        
    - **core**: Core material of the wand (string).
        
    - **length**: Length of the wand (integer).
        
- **patronus**: Patronus of the character (string).
    
- **hogwartsStudent**: Indicates if the character is a Hogwarts student (boolean).
    
- **hogwartsStaff**: Indicates if the character is Hogwarts staff (boolean).
    
- **actor**: Actor portraying the character (string).
    
- **alternate_actors**: Array of alternate actors (array of strings).
    
- **alive**: Indicates if the character is alive (boolean).
    
- **image**: URL of the character's image (string).
    

### Notes

- This endpoint provides a comprehensive list of characters, including their attributes and status.
    
- Ensure that the request is properly authenticated if required by your application.
